entry.title: Washington Post: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Chasiv Yar strike traps dozens under rubble; Ukraine eyes counteroffensive
  • Sri Lanka’s president and prime minister offer to step down, but chaos remains
  • Family of American missing in Ukraine says U.S. response is inadequate
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • A Ukrainian priest splits Sundays between church and the front lines
  • As war nears 5th month, Blinken keeps Russian diplomats at arm’s length
  • Blinken to stop in Tokyo to pay respects to slain leader Abe
  • Sri Lankan president to resign next week, says Parliament speaker, after protesters storm residence
  • Blinken criticizes China’s ‘alignment’ with Russia; divided G-20 ends
  • Japan’s youth baseball culture is brutal. Will Roki Sasaki change that?
  • What to know about the upheaval in Sri Lanka
  • What next for Boris Johnson? Books, columns, speeches, a comeback?
  • In photos and videos: Protesters storm Sri Lankan president’s home
  • Luis Echeverría, Mexican politician with tarnished legacy, dies at 100
  • Cloudburst kills at least 16 on pilgrimage in India’s Kashmir region
  • Outage in Canada leaves millions without internet; ATMs, 911 lines hit
  • Ukraine Muslims pray for victory, end of occupation
  • Iran enriches to 20% with new centrifuges at fortified site
  • South Africa police say 15 killed in bar shooting in Soweto
  • Ukraine: 10 dead in rocket attack on apartment building
  • Bulls spare runners from gorings for 4th day in Pamplona
  • Anxiety grows for Ukraine's grain farmers as harvest begins
  • Luis Echeverria, Mexico leader blamed for massacres, dies
  • Venezuela charges 12 in murder of former guerrilla leader
  • Nicaragua goes after newspaper for reporting nuns' explusion
  • A couple fed nearby ducks. Now, their HOA is threatening to foreclose.
  • Gretchen Whitmer’s abortion fight — from the porch with her daughters
  • Therapy dogs and questions: How Highland Park’s children are coping
  • Japanese Americans shocked by Abe killing: There ‘is no safe place’
  • Without abortion, advocates worry that abuse victims will be trapped
  • Two long weeks: Inside Biden’s struggle to respond to abortion ruling
  • Boris Johnson Exits, But the Damage to the UK Will Linger
  • Putin’s New Weapon of Mass Disruption: Kazakh Oil
  • Biden’s Middle East Trip Fraught With Risk and Opportunity
  • Abe’s Greatest Economic Policy Legacy Is Looking Careworn
  • Why Banks Face Billions in ‘Hung Debt’ as Deals Cool
  • The Post endorses Trone and Parrott in the Maryland 6th District primaries
  • Twitter’s case against India is crucial to the internet’s future
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 10 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

  • Dramatic Jane Eyre needs a little more conversation, a little more action

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 10 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-10 10:41AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong Science Museum’s dinosaur exhibition: what you need to know, from the ‘Big Eight’ skeletons to touching fossils and baby Toni

  • Secretary of State Blinken travels to Thailand as US eyes counter-China moves in Southeast Asia
  • Sources: Abe shooter says he tried to make bomb; former colleagues shocked, called him ‘totally ordinary’
  • ‘Our Mekong is dying’: Locals reel from fish crisis as dams sprout up from Laos to China
  • China warns of increase in disease, pests in rice crops, potentially impacting production across Asia
  • Thailand has legalised cannabis. Now it needs to weed out the loopholes, critics say
  • Marcos must tread cautiously as US and China seek to reset ties with the Philippines
  • Japan votes as polls open in shadow of ex-prime minister Shinzo Abe’s assassination
  • India fines Amnesty International US$8 million after funding probe
  • South Africa gunmen kill 15 people ‘randomly’ at Soweto bar, say police
  • Russian rockets kill 15 in block of flats in Ukraine’s Donbas, say officials
  • Thousands march outside White House demanding Biden do more to defend US abortion rights
  • Ukraine war: Russian military destroys Western weapons amid eastern push
  • Moscow-born Elena Rybakina powers past Ons Jabeur to Wimbledon singles title
  • Techno party with Love Parade founder hits Berlin’s streets
  • Three more UK Tory MPs unveil bid to replace Boris Johnson
  • Volodymyr Zelensky sacks Ukraine’s envoy to Germany, among other ambassadors
  • Date-rape drugs shock at German chancellor’s summer party
  • Russia’s aim to ‘raise true hell’ with deadly shelling overnight, war in spotlight at G20 meeting
  • Smallest flats see brisk demand at first Hong Kong home sale after Xi speech underlining desire for larger homes
  • To bridge divide at heart of US-China tensions, start with global rethink of public sector’s role
  • Carbon capture and storage tech too risky an investment in absence of quality data and low commercial readiness, report finds
  • Law governing cryptocurrency will boost Hong Kong’s leap into virtual asset market
  • Australia woos Hong Kong tourists to help restore pandemic-battered tourism industry, banks on pent-up demand
  • Shenzhen in China becomes first mainland city to make ‘living wills’ into law covering end-of-life care
  • Alopecia areata, Jada Pinkett Smith’s hair-loss condition, now treatable by arthritis and Covid-19 drug, heralding ‘a new era’ for sufferers
  • Are Scientology founder L. Ron Hubbard’s far-fetched tales in China and Asia diaries actually true - at least partly?
  • Why ex-Lonely Planet publisher switched to counselling: Simon Westcott on death, and being abused by a teacher
  • High-end Spanish cooking that respects ingredients and tradition: Hong Kong moves on from molecular gastronomy of El Bulli’s Ferran Adrià
  • In Ukraine ‘everything is so patriotic now’, even fashion, as military T-shirt designs and hoodies with slogans soar in popularity
  • What can America, land of ‘zombie malls’, learn from Hong Kong’s shopping malls?
  • How Running Out of Time, starring Andy Lau and Lau Ching-wan, helped shape the ‘Johnnie To Cinematic Universe’
  • Sex or your phone – which would you rather go without? Expert relationship advice for those who said the former
  • Casanova: world’s greatest lover or debauched sexual predator? Harvard professor puts legendary libertine’s conquests in context
  • Saint Seb! 5 times Sebastian Vettel proved he’s the nicest guy in Formula One: from campaigning for LGBT rights and supporting Ukraine, to reforming motorsports to combat climate change
  • 14 most extravagant celebrity private jets: from Kim Kardashian’s cashmere-clad Kim Air, and Jay-Z and Beyoncé’s Bombardier Challenger, to tech billionaires Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos’ Gulfstream G550s
  • Who is Sheryl Sandberg, Mark Zuckerberg’s right-hand woman? Meta’s COO of 14 years is stepping down from the company – but keeps a mighty net worth of US$1.5 billion
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • Shinzo Abe’s body arrives in Tokyo as Japan mourns

entry.title: Guardian: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live news: 10 killed in strike on Donetsk apartment block; Zelenskiy dismisses ambassadors
  • Penny Mordaunt joins Conservative leadership race – UK politics live
  • Johnny Depp pens song about defamation trial against ex-wife Amber Heard
  • Futura review – fascinating snapshot of Italy’s young adults
  • South Africa: 15 dead after gunmen open fire at bar in Soweto township
  • The Republican party is terrible.. So why may Democrats lose to them this year? | Robert Reicb
  • Owl and Pussycat author Edward Lear’s no-nonsense talent for capturing the moment in drawings
  • Female pilots finally celebrated for crucial role in Battle of Britain
  • Behind Closed Doors by Seth Alexander Thévoz review – entertaining study of London private members’ clubs
  • Exit the internet, enter the metaverse – your online future is in 3D
  • Trump documentary exposes family divisions over Capitol attack
  • Biden’s executive order on abortion is better than nothing. But not much better | Moira Donegan
  • Row over UK child visas as Ukrainian violinist’s three-month wait continues
  • How a parody song about Peru’s ex-president topped Spotify charts
  • Nigel Slater’s recipes for chicken couscous salad, and for watermelon with peach and feta
  • ‘Extinct’ parrots make a flying comeback in Brazil
  • Rebecca Humphries: ‘I felt as if I had a voice again. Maybe one that mattered’
  • We need a new Tory leader with integrity, decency – and a real commitment to levelling up
  • ‘Now other women are free to say what they believe’: researcher who lost her job over transgender tweets
  • James Ellroy: ‘Alcoholics Anonymous was good for hot tub parties in the 70s’
  • It’s not true that everyone’s got a book in them: give writing back to the writers | Stephanie Merritt
  • Pivots, petulance and performance: how Boris Johnson will be remembered in Europe’s capitals
  • Hollywood bids farewell to three greats united by mafia films and friendship
  • Why be a poor version of Germany instead of doing what we do best? | Torsten Bell
  • Growing ‘culture of extremism’ among UK and European police forces, report warns
  • Neil Young and Crazy Horse: Toast review – brooding ‘lost’ set from 2001
  • The waterlily that changed architecture
  • ‘It’s been a hell of a ride’: John McEnroe on learning to lose and being the rock star of tennis
  • TikTok ‘frog army’ stunt could have grave consequences, experts warn
  • She’s aggressive, hungry and resilient, so England must be wary of Ada Hegerberg | Anita Asante
  • Brian and Charles review – a very British robot story
  • Old Tom to Spieth’s renewal: 18 golden and grim moments from 150 years of Open drama
  • Tadej Pogacar flawless but Wout van Aert signals intentions with spectacular start | William Fotheringham
  • The Magnificent Ambersons at 80: Orson Welles’ powerful but cursed drama
  • The ‘Thomas court’: after biding his time, rightwing justice finds his power
  • Full speed ahead: the enduring appeal of the road movie
  • All Down Darkness Wide review – a superb memoir of love and depression

  • British foreign policy was on autopilot – Boris Johnson showed we have choices
  • I fear my parents will disapprove of my new partner
  • Shinzo Abe: police admit security ‘problems’ for former PM as election vote begins
  • Kyrgios and Djokovic set dinner date side bet ahead of Wimbledon clash
  • Labour calls for investigation into Boris Johnson’s private meeting with ex-KGB agent Alexander Lebedev
  • England’s Jill Scott making most of home Euros after long road to the top
  • Jill Roord strikes to rescue draw for holders Netherlands against Sweden
  • Boris Johnson facing accusation he tried to get job for woman claiming affair
  • Chelsea agree £50m deal to sign Raheem Sterling from Manchester City
  • Native American elders recall abuse at US government boarding schools
  • Gomes and Silva rescue Portugal to deny sluggish Switzerland
  • Revealed: officials raised ‘flag’ over Nadhim Zahawi’s tax affairs before he was appointed chancellor

  • Ukraine urges people of Kherson and Zaporizhzhia to evacuate

  • US army general suspended for mocking Jill Biden’s support of abortion rights

  • Priti Patel’s plan to end Channel crossings in disarray as navy threatens to ‘walk away’
